subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Bachelor’s Degree from a four-year university
Studied Communications with a focus on digital media strategy
Completed coursework in marketing, media production, and analytics
Participated in social media management workshops during university

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Job title: Social Media Manager (Media Technical Director/Manager)
Responsible for managing and growing multiple clients’ social media accounts
Leads client engagement and coordinates campaign execution across platforms
Oversees content calendar and ensures brand consistency

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the digital marketing and social media sector
Industry focus is on media, communications, and content creation
Collaborates with clients in entertainment, retail, and lifestyle verticals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Works for a mid-sized marketing agency with approximately 40 employees
The environment is fast-paced and collaborative, with a flat management structure
Frequent cross-functional teamwork between content, design, and client services

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Agency specializes in digital strategy and social media management for small-to-medium businesses
Market segment includes lifestyle brands, influencers, and local businesses
Agency offers services such as content creation, analytics, and influencer partnerships

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 3-5 years of professional experience in social media management
Joined the current agency immediately after graduation
Spent the first year as an assistant and was promoted to manager in year two

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Holds a mid-level management position within the agency
Acts as a key decision-maker for day-to-day account activities
Occasionally mentors new hires and interns on social media best practices

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Manages daily publishing schedules for Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, and Pinterest accounts
Responds to client and follower emails and direct messages
Coordinates with content creators and graphic designers for campaign assets
Conducts interviews for potential new hires and client onboarding
Monitors account analytics and adjusts posting strategies accordingly

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends roughly 40% of time on content creation and scheduling
About 20% is dedicated to client communications and linking clients
15% on analytics review and reporting
10% on conducting interviews for hiring or collaboration
15% handling administrative tasks including emails and phone calls

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Has autonomy to make daily content and posting decisions for assigned accounts
Can approve minor campaign changes without supervisor input
Major campaign direction or budget allocations require team lead approval
Trusted to resolve client queries and minor issues independently

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Creating and scheduling posts on Instagram and TikTok feels intuitive
Responding to emails and DMs is a well-practiced routine
Linking clients for collaborations comes naturally due to strong communication skills
Basic analytics checks and reporting are performed with ease

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Dealing with negative client feedback or social media crises requires careful handling
Designing multi-platform campaigns with tight deadlines can be complex
Integrating new platforms or tools into workflow often presents learning challenges
Coordinating influencer partnerships involves negotiation and detailed planning

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Bulk scheduling of posts across multiple accounts is repetitive
Aggregating and exporting monthly analytics reports is data-heavy
Identifying and removing spam or bot comments can be automated
Monitoring for trending hashtags or keywords could benefit from automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Brainstorming creative campaigns and content ideas is highly engaging
Collaborating with clients and influencers is enjoyable
Routine email triage and spam moderation are considered tedious
Manual data entry for reporting is the least engaging task

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Switching between multiple platforms disrupts workflow efficiency
Lack of seamless integration between analytics tools and content schedulers
Manual report compilation is time-consuming and prone to errors
Coordinating schedules for client meetings often leads to conflicts

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Tasks that align with creative strengths yield higher productivity and satisfaction
Repetitive or low-skill tasks diminish motivation and slow down workflow
Increased proficiency in analytics tools improves both speed and enjoyment
Balancing enjoyable tasks with necessary administrative work is key to sustained output

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Enjoys experimenting with new digital tools and platforms
Views tech learning as essential for career advancement
Actively seeks out tutorials and webinars on social media innovations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Considers self highly adaptable to new social media features and updates
Quick to pick up new analytics or content management tools
Able to transfer skills across similar platforms with minimal friction

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Regularly dedicates time to learning new AI or social media tools after work hours
Participates in agency-sponsored training sessions or knowledge shares
Prioritizes learning tools that directly impact daily workflow

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by the desire to stay competitive in a fast-moving industry
Occasionally hindered by high workload during campaign launches
Prefers tools with clear, immediate relevance to job tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers often share positive experiences with new tools, encouraging adoption
Management supports tech innovation by facilitating access to premium software
Team culture rewards early adopters of productivity-enhancing technologies

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses ChatGBT (ChatGPT) for content ideation and writing captions
Manages posts via Hootsuite and Later for scheduling
Monitors analytics with Sprout Social and native platform insights
Creates visuals using Canva and Adobe Spark

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Relies on Google Workspace for email, calendar, and document collaboration
Uses Slack for internal team communication
Employs Trello to track project progress and content calendars

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient with Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, Pinterest, and Facebook Business Suite
Comfortable using native insights dashboards for analytics
Familiar with scheduling and reporting tools common in digital marketing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally experiences issues syncing scheduled posts between Hootsuite and Instagram
Analytics data sometimes requires manual exporting due to lack of integration with reporting dashboards
Limited cross-platform integration forces manual updates to content calendars

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basics of natural language processing and generative AI
Familiar with terms such as 'large language models', 'prompt engineering', and 'automation'
Keeps up-to-date with AI trends through industry blogs and webinars

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGBT (ChatGPT) daily for drafting and brainstorming social content
Occasionally experiments with Jasper for copywriting
Has tested Canva’s AI-powered design recommendations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses ChatGBT every morning to generate post ideas and draft captions
Employs AI to suggest optimal posting times based on engagement data
Leverages AI-driven analytics to identify trending topics and hashtags

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Saw a 20% increase in engagement after using AI to optimize post timing and hashtags
Encountered off-brand language in AI-generated captions, highlighting the need for manual review
AI sometimes misses subtle cultural nuances, requiring human oversight

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Agency provides monthly knowledge-sharing sessions on new AI tools
Peers often exchange tips on maximizing AI tool efficiency
Internal documentation is available for onboarding new AI-driven processes

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers step-by-step collaboration with AI for content creation
Often starts with independent idea generation, then uses AI to refine drafts
Collaborative prompts help guide AI output to match brand tone

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI as a creative assistant, providing inspiration and initial drafts
Relies on AI for repetitive or brainstorming tasks, while maintaining final edit control
Treats AI as a co-worker for routine analytics and reporting

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Highly receptive to integrating AI-driven processes that improve efficiency
Open to experimenting with new AI features as they are released
Actively suggests AI workflow improvements to management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational AI interfaces for brainstorming and drafting
Uses command-based prompts for data extraction or analytics requests
Finds hybrid interfaces most effective for complex workflow tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Content creation requires nuanced understanding of brand voice and audience
AI is helpful for ideation but final posts depend on social media trends and culture
Tactical scheduling and crisis management rely heavily on experience-based judgment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for generating initial content drafts but always reviews before publishing
Relies on AI analytics for trends, but cross-checks with native platform data
Maintains human oversight for high-stakes or sensitive brand communications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI to handle 60-70% of repetitive or data-driven tasks
Human input is essential for final content approval and crisis response
Sees value in AI-human partnership for optimizing workflow efficiency

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Acceptable for bulk scheduling, analytics, and routine engagement
High automation is threatening for brand messaging or public relations tasks
Requires manual review for anything that impacts client reputation

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: AI enhances creative and analytical skills by reducing repetitive workload
Some manual skills, like data entry, are becoming less important
Critical thinking and brand management skills remain vital despite AI integration

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Prompt engineering and AI query formulation are now key skills
Evaluating AI-generated content for accuracy and brand fit is a new responsibility
Staying updated with AI capabilities is a continuous learning process

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Low concern about AI replacing creative or strategic aspects of the role
Recognizes that routine scheduling and reporting may become fully automated
Believes the human element is irreplaceable for brand voice and crisis management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: All AI-generated posts are reviewed by a human before publishing
Manual approval is required for high-impact campaigns
System allows easy rollback or revision of automated content

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Company has formalized guidelines on responsible AI use
Team meetings now include regular discussion of AI tool updates
Policy encourages experimentation but mandates human oversight for public content

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will continue to transform digital marketing and communications
Sees AI as a positive force for productivity and creativity if used responsibly
Anticipates increased demand for AI literacy across all industries
Expects AI to augment, not replace, most creative roles

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports transparent disclosure when content is AI-generated
Concerned about potential for bias in AI-generated analytics or content
Believes in strict data privacy when using AI tools with client information

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Wishes for better cross-platform AI integration for scheduling and analytics
Desires more advanced AI tools for detecting emerging trends in real time
Hopes for AI features that can auto-adapt tone to specific brand voices

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Expects the role to become more strategic, focusing on campaign ideation and analytics interpretation
Foresees routine tasks being fully automated, freeing time for high-level work
Believes continuous AI upskilling will be essential for career growth

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Wants regular team training on new AI tools and best practices
Recommends developing clear guidelines for AI-generated content approval
Advocates for investment in cross-platform AI integrations to streamline workflow
Encourages a culture of experimentation and feedback around new AI technologies
